functions.name: af
functions.notes: Use `def --wrapped af [...rest] { ^git add --force ...$rest }` in nu to forward arbitrary flags.
functions.summary: Alias for `git add --force` passing all args through.

functions.name: bconfig
functions.notes: Five sequential `bundle config` calls with no branching. Mechanical translation to nu.
functions.summary: Configures Bundler local settings (clean, shared gems, path, bin, jobs).

functions.name: be
functions.notes: [verifier: be runs bundle exec "$@", forwarding all arguments including arbitrary flags (e.g. --gemfile, --path). This is structurally identical to subrepo (git subrepo "$@"), which is correctly classified wrapped-passthrough. A nu port needs def --wrapped to forward unknown flags; simple-wrapper does not use --wrapped and would silently drop flag arguments.]
functions.summary: Short alias for `bundle exec`, passing all args through.

functions.name: bi
functions.notes: Use `def --wrapped bi [...rest] { bundle install ...$rest }` in nu to forward arbitrary flags.
functions.summary: Short alias for `bundle install`, forwarding all arguments.

functions.name: bo
functions.summary: Lists outdated gems in the bundle by delegating to `bundle outdated`.

functions.name: bra
functions.notes: Uses `$@` to forward arbitrary extra flags — in nu use `def --wrapped bra [...rest] { git branch -avv ...$rest }`. Straightforward port.
functions.summary: Lists all git branches verbosely with tracking info (`git branch -avv`).

functions.name: bu
functions.summary: Updates gems in the bundle by delegating to `bundle update`.

functions.name: bv
functions.summary: Displays the version of Bundler by running `bundle --version`.

functions.name: ccc
functions.notes: `cc` is itself a custom zsh function (wraps the claude CLI). In nu: `export def ccc [...rest] { cc --continue ...$rest }`. Requires `cc` to be ported or available as external.
functions.summary: Runs `cc --continue` (Claude Code continue session) forwarding any extra args.

functions.name: ccr
functions.notes: `cc` is a custom zsh autoload function (at ~/.config/zsh/functions/cc); it must be ported or exposed in nu before ccr can call it.
functions.summary: Resumes a Claude Code session by calling `cc --resume` with any extra args.

functions.name: cdown
functions.notes: curl_download exists as a fish function; it must be ported to nu first. Wrapper itself is trivial: `export def cdown [url: string] { curl_download $url }`.
functions.summary: Thin wrapper around curl_download that validates a URL argument is provided.

functions.name: codesign_verify
functions.notes: Long `-R='...'` string with single quotes — must be carefully quoted in nu. Use `def --wrapped` to pass additional flags. The `-R=` flag form should be passed as a single string argument.
functions.summary: Runs codesign --verify with a hardcoded Apple certificate requirement string, forwarding additional args.

functions.name: dc
functions.summary: Show the git staging area diff (git diff --cached).

functions.name: diff
functions.notes: Nu pipe syntax is straightforward; use ^git and ^diff-so-fancy to invoke externals. Declare with def --wrapped to forward arbitrary git flags.
functions.summary: Run git diff piped through diff-so-fancy for colorized output.

functions.name: dir
functions.notes: Straightforward ...rest args passthrough in nu. Note the name 'dir' conflicts with the Windows/DOS 'dir' command but not a nu builtin.
functions.summary: Forcefully removes one or more Docker images via 'docker image rm --force'.

functions.name: ff
functions.notes: Depends on 'fishfiles' which opens ~/.config/fish/; in nu both 'ff' and 'fishfiles' would need porting. The fish config concept doesn't directly apply to nu, so purpose may be repurposed to open ~/.config/nushell/ instead.
functions.summary: Alias for 'fishfiles' — opens the fish dotfiles directory in an editor.

functions.name: fishfiles
functions.notes: Fish-centric purpose; in nu should likely be repurposed or renamed to open ~/.config/nushell/. The 'edit' callee exists as a custom function in both zsh and fish.
functions.summary: Opens ~/.config/fish/ in the editor via the custom 'edit' function.

functions.name: fork
functions.notes: macOS-only ('open' command). Straightforward ...rest args passthrough in nu. Fork.app confirmed present at /usr/local/bin/Fork.
functions.summary: Launches Fork.app on macOS using 'open -a Fork' with optional path arguments.

functions.name: ginit
functions.notes: Trivial port. In nu: export def ginit [...rest] { git init ...$rest }
functions.summary: Thin wrapper around 'git init' passing all arguments through.

functions.name: git_clean
functions.notes: Fixed flags, no args forwarded — pure alias. In nu: alias git_clean = git clean -xffd
functions.summary: Runs 'git clean -xffd' to remove all untracked and ignored files from the working tree.

functions.name: lga
functions.notes: Long --pretty=format string with color codes passes unchanged to git. Use def --wrapped or ...rest to forward extra args.
functions.summary: Show a pretty all-branches git log graph with relative dates and author.

functions.name: list_codesign_identities
functions.notes: Thin wrapper; `$argv` maps to `...rest` in nu. Use `def --wrapped` to forward any extra flags to security.
functions.summary: Lists code-signing identities using `security find-identity -v -p codesigning`.

functions.name: ls-remote
functions.notes: Hyphenated name requires quoting in nu: export def 'ls-remote' [...rest].
functions.summary: Thin wrapper around git ls-remote.

functions.name: nixgc
functions.notes: Straightforward two-command sequence, no logic. Trivial to port as an `export def` in nu.
functions.summary: Runs nix garbage collection (-d) then optimises the nix store.

functions.name: nixtest
functions.notes: Single command, no logic. The `<nixpkgs>` angle-bracket path is a Nix-specific syntax handled by nix-instantiate, not the shell — passes through fine in nu as a quoted string.
functions.summary: Tests a Nix installation by instantiating the hello package from nixpkgs.

functions.name: octodec
functions.notes: Uses `"$@"` for arbitrary flag passthrough — needs `def --wrapped` in nu so flags like `-L` are forwarded verbatim rather than parsed by nu.
functions.summary: SSHes into octodec.local as phatblat, forwarding all arguments to the remote shell.

functions.name: osversion
functions.summary: Prints the macOS product version number via sw_vers.

functions.name: phatmini
functions.summary: SSH shortcut to phatblat@phatmini.local.

functions.name: pp
functions.notes: Body is just `publish phatblat` — a thin alias around the custom publish function. Depends on publish being ported first.
functions.summary: Publishes the current branch to the 'phatblat' remote by calling the custom publish function.

functions.name: root
functions.summary: Print the root path of the current git repository.

functions.name: sa
functions.summary: Alias for git status, forwarding all arguments.

functions.name: sha256
functions.notes: Direct port: `export def sha256 [...files: path] { shasum --algorithm 256 ...$files }`. Validate non-empty files list in nu with `if ($files | is-empty)` guard.
functions.summary: Convenience wrapper for `shasum --algorithm 256` that requires at least one file argument.

functions.name: simclean
functions.notes: Trivial one-liner: `^xcrun simctl delete unavailable`. macOS-only.
functions.summary: Deletes all unavailable Xcode simulators via xcrun simctl.

functions.name: sshtest
functions.notes: Trivial: `^ssh -T git@github.com`. ssh -T returns exit code 1 on success (GitHub says 'Hi user!'), so nu may surface an error; use `do { ^ssh -T git@github.com }` to suppress.
functions.summary: Tests SSH connection to GitHub using ssh -T.

functions.name: subrepo
functions.summary: Thin wrapper that delegates all args to `git subrepo`.

functions.name: surf
functions.notes: windsurf is not found on PATH currently — may not be installed. Logic: if no arg, default to `.`; otherwise pass $argv. In nu: `export def surf [...rest] { if ($rest | is-empty) { windsurf . } else { windsurf ...$rest } }`.
functions.summary: Opens a path (or current dir) in Windsurf editor.

functions.name: suri
functions.notes: Uses `$@` passthrough; in nu use `def --wrapped suri [...rest]` and `^git submodule update --recursive --init ...$rest`.
functions.summary: Initializes and updates git submodules recursively, forwarding extra args.

functions.name: sysinfo
functions.notes: Three sequential external calls with no control flow; mechanical port. macOS-only (sw_vers, system_profiler). Classify as logic only because it's multiple commands, but difficulty is 1. [verifier: The source file contains exactly 3 sequential external calls (uname, sw_vers, system_profiler) with zero conditionals, loops, or branching. There is no logic. 'simple-wrapper' is more accurate than 'logic', though it is a multi-step wrapper. The 'logic' classification implies conditional/loop handling is needed, which is misleading for porters.]
functions.summary: Prints system information: uname, macOS product version, and software data type from system_profiler.

functions.name: tmsnapshots
functions.notes: One-liner: `^tmutil listlocalsnapshots /`. macOS-only. Output could optionally be parsed as structured data in nu.
functions.summary: Lists local Time Machine snapshots via tmutil listlocalsnapshots.

functions.name: untracked
functions.notes: Single git ls-files call with fixed flags; args forwarded. In nu use `def --wrapped` to pass arbitrary extra flags.
functions.summary: Lists untracked files in the current git repo (not in .gitignore).

functions.name: usage
functions.notes: Straightforward: `du -hs` with optional path arg. In nu, default arg syntax needed (`...rest` or optional param with default '.').
functions.summary: Shows disk usage for a given directory (defaults to current directory).

functions.name: user.email
functions.notes: Function name contains a dot — nu def names support dots but verify no parsing issues. Use `def --wrapped` or `...rest` with `^git config user.email ...$rest`.
functions.summary: Wrapper around `git config user.email` passing all args through.

functions.name: vi
functions.summary: Aliases vi to nvim.

functions.name: vim
functions.summary: Alias vim to nvim

functions.name: warpify
functions.notes: Uses printf with an escape sequence; in nu use `print` with the same escape string. The \x9c (ST) byte may need explicit Unicode escaping in nu.
functions.summary: Prints Warp terminal escape sequence to warpify a subshell

functions.name: xcsp
functions.summary: Prints the path of the currently selected Xcode developer directory

functions.name: aws_test
functions.notes: Fish 'if test -z' maps cleanly to nu 'if ($profile_name | is-empty)'. Default argument pattern is straightforward with nu optional params.
functions.summary: Test AWS credentials by calling sts get-caller-identity with an optional profile.

functions.name: bak
functions.notes: String suffix matching: zsh `${file%.bak}` becomes `$file | str replace --regex '\.bak$' ''` or `path parse` + `path join` in nu. `mv` is a nu builtin (`mv`). Emoji in echo strings are fine. Difficulty 1 — mechanical port.
functions.summary: Toggles .bak extension on a file — adds it if missing, removes it if already present.

functions.name: bigfiles
functions.notes: Pipeline `du -ka . | sort -n -r | head -n 10` can be rewritten as native nu: `du --bytes . | sort-by apparent --reverse | first 10`. Note `du` output semantics differ — nu's `du` command returns structured data. Consider using `^du -ka .` then parse, or fully native approach.
functions.summary: Lists the 10 largest files in the current directory tree by KB size.

functions.name: binstall
functions.notes: Command substitution `$(brew --prefix)` becomes `(brew --prefix | str trim)` in nu. Extra args forwarding needs `...rest` spread. gem and brew both present on PATH.
functions.summary: Installs the Bundler gem with bindir set to `brew --prefix`/bin, forwarding extra args.

functions.name: bq
functions.notes: Arg-splitting on `--` requires manual iteration in nu (no zsh `${array[@]}` equivalent). Consider using nu's native `from json` instead of jq to avoid the external dep entirely. Nushell has no direct `--` splitter idiom; use a positional parameter or a flag.
functions.summary: Queries Homebrew formula info as JSON, optionally piping through a jq filter; splits args on `--` separator.

functions.name: brew_active_version
functions.notes: Calls custom function `bq` (zsh autoload at ~/.config/zsh/functions/bq). Uses `string replace` to strip quotes — in nu use `str replace --all '"' ''` or parse JSON natively with `from json`. `bq $formula | jq .[0].linked_keg` pipeline needs restructuring. Verify `bq` nu callee port exists first.
functions.summary: Returns the active linked keg version for a given Homebrew formula using bq and jq.

functions.name: brew_test
functions.notes: Fish `and`-chained commands become `&&` pipelines or semicolons with `if` guards in nu. `brew ls --versions` exit code check needs `(brew ls --versions $token | complete).exit_code`. `switch` with `case '*'` maps to nu `match _ =>`.
functions.summary: Installs and tests a Homebrew formula from source, with special pre-handling for the mas formula.

functions.name: chexe
functions.notes: Glob default `*.sh` in nu requires `ls *.sh | get name` or glob pattern. Nu does not expand bare globs in strings; must use `glob "*.sh"` explicitly when falling back to default.
functions.summary: Runs chmod +x on provided files, defaulting to *.sh if no args given.

functions.name: cont
functions.notes: Uses `-e .git/MERGE_HEAD` file-existence tests — in nu use `('.git/MERGE_HEAD' | path exists)`. Redirects stdout to /dev/null with `> /dev/null` — in nu use `| ignore` or `null-device`. `$@` passthrough on cherry-pick needs `...rest`. Assumes CWD is git repo root.
functions.summary: Continues an in-progress git operation (merge, rebase, cherry-pick, or am) by detecting which HEAD file exists.

functions.name: ddc
functions.notes: All four callees (dcp, dvp, dip, dnp) are custom functions in both zsh and fish; they must be ported first.
functions.summary: Docker deep clean — runs dcp, dvp, dip, and dnp in sequence to prune containers, volumes, images, and networks.

functions.name: ddd
functions.notes: Nu env var access: `$env.DERIVED_DATA`. Use `path exists` instead of `test -d`. Fish version calls a custom `error` function for messaging — zsh version uses plain `echo`. Use `rm -rf` or nu's `rm --recursive --force`.
functions.summary: Deletes the Xcode DerivedData directory pointed to by $DERIVED_DATA.

functions.name: defaults_set
functions.notes: Mostly a long sequential list of `defaults write` calls — mechanical to port but very long. Some referenced apps (Tweetbot, System Preferences as distinct from System Settings on macOS 13+) may be obsolete. `mdutil -i off /Volumes/ThunderBox` references a specific volume name. No logic/conditionals beyond the sequential list; difficulty is 2 only due to length and sudo calls. In nu, `sudo` invocations work as external commands.
functions.summary: Bulk-sets macOS system preferences (Finder, Dock, keyboard, Spotlight, Safari, Xcode, etc.) via `defaults write`.

functions.name: derived_data
functions.notes: Unlike cargo_target, this does NOT export an env var (no `export DERIVED_DATA`), but note CLAUDE.md documents a $DERIVED_DATA env var used by ddd — presumably set elsewhere. Icon file path contains spaces (`Agua Onyx Icons/Onyx Media Drive.png`) — must be quoted correctly in nu. `ramdisk` is only a custom function in fish, not zsh; verify it exists in nu before porting. Fish's `list $output` appears to be non-standard — zsh uses `echo $output`.
functions.summary: Mounts a 10 GB RAM disk named DerivedData for Xcode and sets a custom icon on it.

functions.name: dotfiles
functions.notes: Calls custom `edit` function (exists at ~/.config/zsh/functions/edit and ~/.config/fish/functions/edit.fish). References `~/.dotfiles/` which may not be the current dotfiles repo path (the repo root is `~`). The switch/case maps to nu `match`. Path `~/.dotfiles/` looks like a legacy location — verify it still applies. `test -d ~/.config/$type` maps to `($'~/.config/($type)' | path exists)` in nu.
functions.summary: Opens dotfiles or config subdirectories in the editor, dispatching by type argument.

functions.name: find_file
functions.notes: Straightforward logic with argument validation. In nu use named params: `def find_file [file_name: string, base_dir?: string = "."]`. `2>/dev/null` stderr redirect becomes `| ignore-errors` or `do { find ... } | complete`. `find` glob pattern `*$file_name*` needs interpolation care in nu strings.
functions.summary: Finds files by name using both mdfind (Spotlight) and find, with an optional base directory argument.

functions.name: fish_logo
functions.notes: Fish set_color must be replaced with nu 'ansi' command. Fish $o/$m/$i shortcut variables need nu let bindings. The fish-specific 'contains --' idiom maps to 'in' operator in nu. Consider whether this logo is useful outside fish.
functions.summary: Print a colorful ASCII-art Fish shell logo with configurable colors.

functions.name: flushdns
functions.notes: Calls custom 'user_is_admin' function (exists in both zsh and fish). In nu, use 'if (user_is_admin) == false' with proper exit-code checking. The '&&' chaining in zsh maps to sequential nu statements with early return on error. 'return 1' maps to 'return' with error in nu.
functions.summary: Flushes the macOS DNS cache after verifying the user is an admin.

functions.name: gem_install
functions.notes: Uses `$(brew --prefix)/bin` via command substitution — in nu: `(^brew --prefix | str trim)/bin`. Requires argument validation (empty check) before calling gem. `shift; "$@"` pattern for rest args maps to splitting `...rest` in nu.
functions.summary: Installs a Ruby gem with --force, placing the binary in the Homebrew prefix bin directory.

functions.name: hgrep
functions.notes: In nu, history is structured: `history | where command =~ $pattern` is the idiomatic rewrite. The zsh `history` command output format differs from nu's structured history table. Nu's `history | find` or `where` avoids grep entirely.
functions.summary: Greps the shell command history for a pattern.

functions.name: ip
functions.notes: Calls custom fish function 'en1' (wraps ifconfig en1). Pipeline rewrite: ^ifconfig en1 | lines | where $it =~ 'inet ' | split column ' ' | get column2.0. en1 must also be ported or inlined. macOS-specific (en1 interface). [verifier: ip.fish calls en1 (a custom callee, correctly listed), then pipes through grep and awk. ifconfig is never called directly by ip.fish — it is called inside en1.fish. Listing ifconfig as a direct external_dep of ip is inaccurate; it is a transitive dep via the en1 callee.]
functions.summary: Show the IPv4 address for the en1 network interface.

functions.name: jq
functions.notes: Reads all stdin into a variable then passes to 'command jq'; on non-zero exit, prints the original input to stderr. In nu: use 'let input = $in', run '^jq ...$rest' via try/catch, print $input to stderr on error. The zsh function uses 'command jq' to bypass itself — in nu use '^jq'. 2>/dev/null stderr suppression needs care. The 'which jq' returns the zsh autoload wrapper (the function itself), not a separate binary — the real binary exists via mise/brew.
functions.summary: Wrapper around jq that buffers stdin, runs jq, and prints the original input to stderr on failure.

functions.name: jv
functions.notes: java -version writes to stderr; fish captures it with (java -version 2>&1). In nu: ^java --version 2>&1 | ... or use do { ^java -version } | complete | get stderr. String splitting and quote-stripping maps to nu's 'split row' and 'str replace'. fish 'string split' with index access needs care in nu (split row returns a list).
functions.summary: Displays the installed Java version number by parsing 'java -version' stderr output.

functions.name: license
functions.notes: Heredoc string interpolation in nu uses different syntax. The copyright symbol emoji in the heredoc may need care with nu string escaping. `date` formatting needs to use `date now | format date "%Y"` in nu. `cat >>` appends — nu has no built-in append-to-file shorthand, use `open | append` or `save --append`.
functions.summary: Writes an MIT LICENSE.md file with current year and appends a license section to README.md, then git-commits both files.

functions.name: list
functions.notes: Purely shell logic with `printf '%s\n'`. In nu, args are already structured so `...rest` params printed with `each { |it| print $it }` or returned as a list. The `-s` flag handling (shift) maps to nu flag parsing. Name 'list' does not shadow a nu builtin (confirmed). Stderr redirects (`>&2`) map to `| to text | print --stderr`.
functions.summary: Prints each argument on its own line, with an optional -s flag to skip the first arg as a label.

functions.name: maslink
functions.notes: Fish switch/case on hostname result must become nu match. error and masshow are custom fish callees. Hardcoded hostname list is brittle. nu has no built-in `test -L`; use `($destination | path type) == symlink`. Return-code propagation from rm differs in nu.
functions.summary: Creates or removes a symlink from a Xcode DerivedData mas debug build into ~/bin, hostname-dispatched.

functions.name: masrm
functions.notes: Fish `type -q` becomes `which` or `(which mas | is-empty)` in nu. `$USER` becomes `$env.USER`. Multiple custom callees (error, fileowner, masshow) must also be ported. Return codes map to `return` or `error make` in nu.
functions.summary: Uninstalls the mas binary, choosing between trash (root-owned) or brew uninstall (user-owned), with error reporting.

functions.name: masshow
functions.notes: Fish `which` becomes `(which mas)` in nu (which returns a string). `ll` is a custom callee. `type -q` check maps to `which` + error handling in nu.
functions.summary: Shows which copy of mas is active: lists it with ll, lipo arch info, and mas version.

functions.name: moj_host
functions.notes: Fish `string match`/`string split`/`string sub` need nu equivalents (`str contains`, `split row`, `str substring`). Fish switch glob patterns like `'labtop*'` must become `match` patterns in nu. `>/dev/null` redirect not needed in nu (suppress with `| ignore`).
functions.summary: Prints an emoji representing the current hostname, matching known machine names with a switch/case.

functions.name: new
functions.notes: Uses custom `rev-parse` callee (wrapper around git rev-parse). Fish `set -x` (export) inside function just sets a local — nu equivalent is `let`. The `$argv` passthrough maps to `..rest` in nu. String interpolation for `$commit'@{1}'` needs care: nu uses `$"($commit)@{1}"`.
functions.summary: Lists new git commits introduced since the last pull, using reflog positions @{1} and @{0}.

functions.name: openports
functions.notes: The grep text filter is a candidate for nu structured pipeline: `lsof -i | lines | where ($it | str contains 'LISTEN')`, or use `lsof -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN` to avoid grep entirely. Straightforward but benefits from native nu filtering.
functions.summary: Lists open listening ports for the current user via lsof -i | grep LISTEN.

functions.name: ox
functions.notes: Uses a bare glob `*.xcodeproj` before $argv. In nu, glob expansion is not automatic in command arguments; use `glob '*.xcodeproj'` and spread the result. Also nu's `open` is a builtin that reads files — the macOS `open` binary must be called as `^open` to bypass the nu builtin.
functions.summary: Opens a *.xcodeproj in the current directory with the open command.

functions.name: path_show
functions.notes: References fish-specific `$fish_user_paths` which has no direct nu equivalent; in nu use `$env.PATH` (a list). The `list` callee must also be ported. Replace with a nu-native structured approach iterating `$env.PATH`.
functions.summary: Pretty-prints fish_user_paths and PATH using the custom list function.

functions.name: pborigin
functions.notes: Line 11 calls `remote rename origin phatblat` without the `git` prefix — this looks like a bug in the source (missing `git`). The `rv` callee is invoked in both branches and must be ported. In nu, capture `git remote | lines` instead of fish `contains`. Use `if 'phatblat' in (git remote | lines)` idiom. [verifier: /Users/phatblat/.config/fish/functions/remote.fish exists and is a custom function (wraps `git remote $argv`). Line 11 `remote rename origin phatblat` is NOT a bug — it calls this custom wrapper, not a bare `git` subcommand. The original classification missed `remote` as a callee and misdiagnosed the line as a missing `git` prefix.]
functions.summary: Renames the git 'origin' remote to 'phatblat' if not already present.

functions.name: pcopy
functions.notes: In nu: `pwd | str trim | pbcopy`. The zsh version pipes through xargs echo -n to strip the newline; nu's string pipeline handles that cleanly.
functions.summary: Copies the current working directory path to the macOS clipboard.

functions.name: pip
functions.notes: Uses command substitution `(brew --prefix)` inline in env var assignment. In nu, must capture `brew --prefix` output first with `let pfx = (^brew --prefix)`, then set env vars in the call scope using `with-env`. Needs def --wrapped to forward unknown pip flags.
functions.summary: Wraps pip with CFLAGS/LDFLAGS set to Homebrew include/lib paths for native extension builds.

functions.name: plcat
functions.notes: Error return codes (return 1, return 2) map to nu 'error make'. Pipeline ^plutil | ^bat is fine in nu. macOS-only (plutil).
functions.summary: Convert a plist file to XML and display it with bat syntax highlighting.

functions.name: provisioning_print
functions.notes: Uses zsh `[[ -z ]]` and `[[ ! -e ]]` tests; nu equivalents are `str is-empty` and `path exists`. Stderr redirection `2>/dev/null` must become `| complete` or `do { ... } | ignore` in nu. `return 1/2` become `error make` or just `return` with exit code handling.
functions.summary: Decodes and prints a .mobileprovision file as readable XML using security cms.

functions.name: psgrep
functions.notes: Classic grep-self-exclusion pipeline. In nu: `ps | where name =~ $pattern` — no need for the grep-exclusion trick since nu `ps` returns structured data. Rewrite as native nu pipeline for idiomatic output.
functions.summary: Greps the ps aux output for a given process name, filtering out the grep process itself.

functions.name: publish
functions.notes: Argument validation (empty check) maps cleanly to nu; use 'error make' for usage error. git rev-parse subshell becomes (^git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD | str trim).
functions.summary: Push the current branch to a named remote with upstream tracking (-u).

functions.name: pull_ssh_config
functions.notes: Hostname check `test phatmini = (hostname)` translates to `(sys host).hostname | str contains 'phatmini'` or `^hostname | str trim` comparison in nu. The `~` path expansion in `~/.ssh/config` works in nu but must use string interpolation carefully.
functions.summary: Copies SSH config from phatmini.c4 to local ~/.ssh/config, guarded against running on phatmini itself.

functions.name: ra
functions.notes: Zsh version uses sed for string manipulation (`sed 's|.*/||'`); fish version uses `string split` - both straightforward to rewrite with nu's `str replace`/`split`. Callees rv, fetch, error need nu ports. Conditional URL-building logic is the main complexity. [verifier: source_used is the zsh version. That file contains zero custom-function calls: it uses only external git and sed. The listed callees [rv, fetch, error] appear in the fish version (ra.fish), not the zsh source. The fish version also calls remote_for_current_branch, which is absent from the list. Correct callees for the zsh source are empty.]
functions.summary: Adds a git remote; infers the URL from the current branch's upstream remote when only a name is given.

functions.name: ramdisk
functions.notes: Fish `math` becomes nu arithmetic (`$disk_size * 1024 * 2048`). `string trim` on hdiutil output becomes `| str trim`. The fish `and` combinator after hdiutil translates to `if ($status == 0)` or nu's `try` block. `$status` check translates to nu's `$env.LAST_EXIT_CODE` or `| complete | get exit_code`.
functions.summary: Creates a macOS RAM disk of a given size (GB) and formats it as HFS+.

functions.name: rewrite
functions.notes: Arg-count validation and regex check (`=~ ^[0-9]+$`) need nu equivalents (`$in | str contains`, `str length`, or `$in =~ '\d+'`). Dynamic array construction for --refs flag must use list spreading. Zsh `>&2` stderr becomes `print --stderr` or `eprintln` in nu. `command -v` check becomes `which git-filter-repo | is-empty`.
functions.summary: Rewrites git commit author/committer email or name using git-filter-repo with optional commit-count limit.

functions.name: sethostname
functions.notes: user_is_admin and error are custom fish callees that must be ported first. sudo calls work in nu. Fish `not user_is_admin` becomes `if not (user_is_admin)` in nu.
functions.summary: Sets macOS system hostname (ComputerName, HostName, LocalHostName, NetBIOSName) via sudo.

functions.name: signing_cert_details
functions.notes: Pipeline: `security | openssl` is straightforward in nu using `^security ... | ^openssl ...`. Input validation with usage message to stderr; use `eprintln` in nu. Return 1 on missing arg maps to `error make`.
functions.summary: Prints signing certificate details by piping security find-certificate output through openssl x509 -text.

functions.name: skip
functions.notes: Shadows nu builtin `skip` (list/table skipper). Must be named differently in nu or namespaced (e.g. `git skip`). Chained `||` with stderr redirects: in nu use try/catch or check exit codes with `do { ^git rebase --skip } | complete`. 2>/dev/null maps to `| ignore` or capturing stderr via `complete`.
functions.summary: Skips the current commit in a git rebase, cherry-pick, or am operation by trying each in order.

functions.name: sshkeyfingerprint
functions.notes: Calls custom `sshkey` callee; must be ported first. Uses command substitution with error propagation (`if ! file=$(sshkey)`); in nu use `let file = (sshkey); if $file == null { return }` or error handling. `echo -n` (no newline) maps to `print -n` in nu.
functions.summary: Shows the MD5 fingerprint of an SSH public key, defaulting to the key returned by the sshkey function.

functions.name: sshnewkey
functions.notes: Simple conditional default: `let comment = if ($comment | is-empty) { $"($env.USER)@(^hostname)" } else { $comment }`. No tricky semantics.
functions.summary: Generates a new ed25519 SSH key at ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 with an optional comment defaulting to USER@hostname.

functions.name: unstage
functions.notes: Simple if/else on arg count. In nu: `if ($args | is-empty) { git restore --staged . } else { git restore --staged ...$args }`. Straightforward port.
functions.summary: Unstages files from the git index (git restore --staged), defaulting to all if no args given.

functions.name: untar
functions.notes: Zsh `${file_name%%.*}` (greedy strip all extensions) needs nu `path parse` + custom logic since `path basename` only strips last extension. `[[ ! -f ]]` becomes `not ($1 | path exists)` or checking `path type`. Numbered return codes must be preserved.
functions.summary: Extracts a .tar.gz archive into a new directory named after the archive (strips all extensions).

functions.name: upstall
functions.notes: The wrapper itself is trivial, but the callee ⏫_upstall is a complex fish-specific pipeline with emoji-named functions (⬆️_upmodule, 🗄_gitconfig, etc.). Nu cannot use emoji identifiers with the same ease; the entire callee tree needs porting. Wrapper can be a simple `export def upstall [...rest] { ⏫_upstall ...$rest }` once the callee exists.
functions.summary: Thin alias/wrapper that delegates to the emoji-named ⏫_upstall function which orchestrates system-wide updates across brew, npm, pip, rustup, etc.

functions.name: upstreamify
functions.notes: Calls two custom functions: `remote` (a thin wrapper over `git remote`) and `rv` (likely lists remotes verbosely). Both must be ported before this function. The missing `git` prefix is intentional — `remote` is a custom callee. Port as: `^git remote rename origin upstream; rv`.
functions.summary: Renames the git remote 'origin' to 'upstream', then calls rv to display remotes.

functions.name: user.name
functions.notes: The dot in the function name `user.name` is invalid in nu (dots are record-access operators). Must be renamed (e.g. `git-user-name` or `user_name`). Uses `$@` passthrough — use `def --wrapped` in nu. Difficulty 2 due to naming conflict.
functions.summary: Gets or sets the git user.name configuration value, forwarding all args.

functions.name: user_is_admin
functions.notes: Calls custom `is_mac` callee. macOS path uses dsmemberutil and string comparison; Linux path uses `groups | grep -q adm` pipeline. In nu: `^dsmemberutil checkmembership -U $env.USER -G admin | str contains 'user is a member'` for macOS; `^groups | str contains adm` for Linux. Return value is a boolean exit code — nu functions should return bool explicitly.
functions.summary: Tests whether the current user is a member of the admin group, supporting both macOS and Linux.

functions.name: wip
functions.notes: Calls custom fish functions git_repo_clean and error which must be ported to nu first. In nu, error handling uses `error make` rather than a custom error function. Return codes map to `return` in nu.
functions.summary: Stages all changes and commits with a WIP message

functions.name: xc
functions.notes: Uses zsh mapfile + process substitution to collect find results into array. In nu, use `^find ... | lines` to get a list. Array indexing is 1-based in zsh but 0-based in nu. `open` is also a nu builtin — the external `open` must be called as `^open` in nu.
functions.summary: Opens the Xcode workspace, project, or Package.swift in the current directory

functions.name: xclist
functions.notes: Uses -t 1 tty check to decide output format; nu has no direct equivalent — use `$env.TERM` or always print structured data. ANSI coloring can be done with `ansi` command in nu. Glob expansion in array context needs `ls` + filtering in nu. readlink has no direct nu builtin; use `^readlink`.
functions.summary: Lists all installed Xcode versions with colorized symlink info

functions.name: xcswitch
functions.notes: Uses `pushd`/`popd` for directory change — needs_env is false since it only changes dir transiently inside the function. In nu use `cd` inside a block or pass the path to `ln`. `xcss`, `xclist`, `xcsp` are custom zsh functions that must also be ported. `head -1` on xcsp output should become `| first`. obsolete_risk=maybe because xcss/xclist/xcsp rely on xcode-select tooling that may not be present on all machines.
functions.summary: Switches the active Xcode version by symlinking the chosen Xcode-<version>.app to Xcode.app and running xcss.

functions.name: xcvall
functions.notes: Calls custom zsh function pkginfo (which wraps pkgutil). pkginfo must be ported to nu first. mdfind is macOS-only but is present on the system. Output is printed to stdout; nu version can collect mdfind output with `^mdfind ...`.
functions.summary: Lists all installed Xcode versions via Spotlight and CLI tools package info

functions.name: ap
functions.notes: git add --patch launches an interactive TUI. Nu must run it as an external with stdio inherited (^git add --patch ...rest); no nu-native equivalent possible.
functions.summary: Interactively stage hunks with git add --patch.

functions.name: apv
functions.notes: cd must persist: use `def --env apv []`. lg10 is a custom zsh callee that must be ported. git clone/pull error handling: check `$env.LAST_EXIT_CODE`. `cd ~/dev || return` pattern becomes `if not ($apv_dir | path exists) { cd ~/dev; git clone ... } else { cd $apv_dir; git pull }`.
functions.summary: Navigates to (or clones) the ApplePlatformVersions repo, pulls latest, and shows log.

functions.name: br
functions.notes: The fish function writes broot's output command to a tmpfile and sources it; in nu this requires def --env, capturing broot --outcmd output and executing the cd. The symlink at ~/.config/fish/functions/br.fish is broken (target path missing) but broot binary is present and can regenerate the launcher. Use broot --print-shell-function nushell if supported, otherwise hand-port.
functions.summary: broot launcher that captures the output command (e.g. cd) and sources it so directory changes persist in the caller shell.

functions.name: brew_home
functions.notes: Uses `export BREW_HOME=...` to persist across calls — needs `def --env` in nu. `(($+commands[brew]))` zsh idiom becomes `(which brew | is-not-empty)` or `(do { ^brew --version } | complete | get exit_code) == 0`. `is_mac`/`is_linux` are custom functions that must also be ported.
functions.summary: Returns the Homebrew prefix path (or prefix/opt/<formula>), setting and caching $BREW_HOME in the environment.

functions.name: bundle-pull
functions.notes: Hardcoded hostname lookup (protop/phatmini) makes this machine-specific. `realpath --relative-to` is GNU coreutils; macOS `realpath` may not support `--relative-to` — use `python3 -c` or `grealpath`. Nu `$status` is per-command via `$env.LAST_EXIT_CODE`; use `(cmd | complete).exit_code`. Function name contains a hyphen — valid in nu as `def 'bundle-pull'`. Calls several custom callees that must be ported first.
functions.summary: Copies a dirty git working copy from a remote machine via SSH+bundle, applies it locally, then cleans up.

functions.name: cargo_target
functions.notes: `export CARGO_TARGET_DIR` must become `$env.CARGO_TARGET_DIR = ...` inside `def --env`. `ramdisk` is a custom function only in fish (not in zsh functions dir) — verify it is ported first. Fish `list $output` (not a standard command) appears to be a local idiom; zsh uses `echo $output`. Nu needs `def --env` to propagate env to caller.
functions.summary: Mounts a RAM disk named CargoTarget and sets CARGO_TARGET_DIR to it; optionally quiet.

functions.name: gpv
functions.notes: env-mutating because 'nav' uses pushd to change directory. In nu, needs 'def --env' and each callee (nav, clone, pull, lg10) must also be ported first. The function is project-specific (GooglePlatformVersions) so may be obsolete if that project is no longer active. All callees confirmed as custom fish functions.
functions.summary: Navigates to (or clones) the GooglePlatformVersions repo, pulls latest, and shows git log.

functions.name: home
functions.notes: In nu requires 'def --env home [] { cd ~ }'. Trivial but must use def --env for cd to persist.
functions.summary: Changes the current directory to the home directory (~).

functions.name: jdk
functions.notes: jdk_set mutates JAVA_HOME and PATH (env-mutating); the whole jdk command therefore needs def --env. The 'set' and 'studio' subcommands delegate to jdk_set which calls path_add - all callee functions need nu ports first. Case dispatch is straightforward but callee dependency chain is non-trivial.
functions.summary: Manages JDK selection: lists installed JDKs, sets JAVA_HOME/PATH via jdk_set, or shows current JDK info.

functions.name: masd
functions.notes: Uses pushd for directory navigation — nu requires `def --env` and `cd`. clone_or_pull is a custom callee that also needs porting. `$HOME` expands as `$env.HOME` in nu.
functions.summary: Clones or pulls the mas-cli/mas repo into ~/dev/mas-cli/mas, then pushd into it.

functions.name: mdp
functions.notes: Fish `pushd` becomes `cd` in nu with `def --env`. Trivial port.
functions.summary: Quick navigation to ~/dev/mdp directory.

functions.name: mkdir
functions.notes: Shadows nu builtin mkdir. Nu's mkdir does not cd afterward; needs def --env to persist the cd. Must detect trailing flag args (e.g. -p) to avoid cd-ing into a flag value. nu mkdir returns a table, so calling `^mkdir` (external) or just `mkdir` then `cd` in a def --env is the cleanest approach.
functions.summary: Creates a directory with -p and then cds into the last non-flag argument.

functions.name: mpv
functions.notes: Uses `cd` so needs `def --env`. The `[[ ! -d ]]` conditional becomes `if not ($mpv_dir | path exists)` in nu. `lg10` is a custom callee that must also be ported. Note: the function is named `mpv` but that name collides with the mpv media player binary — check for conflicts.
functions.summary: Clones or pulls the MicrosoftPlatformVersions repo and opens it in lg10 (lazygit).

functions.name: rl
functions.notes: Target directory ~/dev/www/reflog/www does not exist on this machine — obsolete_risk is maybe (may be intentional for other machines). In nu, `cd` with `def --env` is the equivalent of pushd for persistent directory change. Nu has no pushd/popd stack natively; if stack behavior is needed, it must be emulated.
functions.summary: Navigates (pushd) to ~/dev/www/reflog/www.

functions.name: shell_choose
functions.notes: Uses fish `read --prompt-str` for interactive input; nu equivalent is `input`. Calls three custom callees (brew_home, shell_add, shell_switch) which must all be ported. The `grep -q` check on /etc/shells translates to `open /etc/shells | str contains $new_shell_path`. `$SHELL` maps to `$env.SHELL` in nu.
functions.summary: Interactively prompts the user to change their default login shell.

functions.name: toggle_wait
functions.notes: Exports EDITOR or VISUAL so must use `def --env`. Uses `${state:l}` (lowercase operator) — nu equivalent is `str downcase`. References custom env vars WAIT_FLAG, EDITOR_CLI, EDITOR_GUI, WAIT_FLAG_CLI, WAIT_FLAG_GUI that must be defined in nu env. Case statement maps cleanly to nu match.
functions.summary: Toggles or sets the wait flag on VISUAL or EDITOR environment variables, reading from WAIT_FLAG/EDITOR_CLI/EDITOR_GUI/WAIT_FLAG_CLI/WAIT_FLAG_GUI env vars.

functions.name: xcv
functions.notes: Multiple grep/sed/awk pipelines parsing plutil -p output; best rewritten using `^plutil -extract` (plist key extraction) or parsed as structured data in nu. Large case/esac block mapping build numbers to beta labels maps cleanly to nu `match`. The `${active_version/Developer/}` string substitution needs `str replace`. The -s short flag requires nu parameter handling. `plutil -p` output is human-readable text, not JSON — consider `plutil -convert json -o -` instead for structured parsing.
functions.summary: Displays the marketing and build version of the currently selected Xcode
